title: IPC client trusts pre-existing /tmp socket without owner or symlink verification

findingDetails.evidence: ensureSocketEnvironment() sets/uses SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR, mkdirSyncs it, then only checks statSync(socketDir).mode for group/world-writable at lines 753-762; it does not lstat the directory or require uid ownership. The bridge resolves the env path directly in .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:80-87, and probeDaemon accepts any JSON-RPC initialize result/error as alive at lines 145-216. callTool then uses that socket for initialize/tools/call at spec-memory-cli.ts:924-946.
findingDetails.why: The default socket directory is /tmp/mk-spec-memory in .opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs:103-110. On a shared host, a local attacker can pre-create that path as a symlink to an attacker-controlled non-writable directory containing daemon-ipc.sock. Because the client does not reject symlinked/foreign-owned socket dirs before the deep probe, a fake daemon can answer initialize, suppress cold spawn even in normal mode, receive tool arguments, and spoof CLI results.
findingDetails.recommendation: Before probing or connecting, centralize a client-side IPC perimeter check: lstat the final socket directory and reject symlinks, require current uid ownership when process.getuid exists, reject group/world-writable dirs, lstat the socket node if present and require a real socket owned by the current uid. Mirror the HF model-server assertSocketDirOwnership/per-node checks or the shared socket-server ownership fence, and apply it in the shim/CLI bridge path before probeDaemon/connect.
